user_message: Just came out of QBR preparation with Alpine Ski House. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-12 15:58 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 15:37 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 13:50 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Champion-Change Email Signal -> Re-engagement Plan template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
